pattern: A valid binary tree has exactly one root (no parent), every non-root node has exactly one parent, and there are no cycles — union-find or parent-tracking with a reachability BFS/DFS confirms all three.
hints: Which node should be the root? It must be the unique node that no other node claims as a child.
If more than one node has no parent, or if any node has two parents, the structure is invalid. Cycle detection catches any remaining violation.
Track in-degree for every node. The root is the single node with in-degree 0. Then BFS/DFS from the root and verify every node is visited exactly once.

edge_inputs.description: cycle: node points back to itself indirectly (invalid)
two disconnected valid trees — fails because not one tree
multiple roots — no single parent-free node dominates

stress.description: large valid random binary tree built top-down
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    left = [-1] * n
    right = [-1] * n
    # build a valid tree by assigning children level-by-level
    available = list(range(1, n))
    rng.shuffle(available)
    frontier = [0]
    idx = 0
    while frontier and idx < len(available):
        node = frontier.pop(rng.randint(0, len(frontier) - 1) if len(frontier) > 1 else 0)
        if idx < len(available):
            left[node] = available[idx]; frontier.append(available[idx]); idx += 1
        if idx < len(available):
            right[node] = available[idx]; frontier.append(available[idx]); idx += 1
    return [n, left, right]
